page: Microsoft Azure FX8-4ms_v2 is a memory-intensive virtual machine from the FXmsv2 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 4 vCPUs, derived from 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The processor operates at a base clock speed of 4.2 GHz and includes a 260 MB L3 cache. This instance is equipped with 168.0 GB of RAM, yielding an exceptionally high memory-to-core ratio of 42.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, but it is premium storage capable.

Benchmark data indicates strong single-core performance, highlighted by top-tier Geekbench single-core and text processing scores. Single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression also register strong results. However, multi-core performance is comparatively limited, with stress-ng multi-core and bzip3 multi-threaded decompression scoring in the lower percentiles due to the 4 vCPU constraint. Memory bandwidth for small cached blocks is average at 345.37 GB/sec, but drops to a weaker 36.1 GB/sec for large uncached blocks. Database operations and Redis SET performance remain in the average tier, processing 6.27 million operations per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the FX8-4ms_v2 offers a specialized resource profile. It provides a massive memory footprint relative to its compute capacity, making it highly suitable for memory-bound workloads that do not require massive parallel processing. The primary tradeoff is the low physical core count, which limits its effectiveness for highly parallelized tasks like software compilation or large-scale multi-threaded decompression. This server is best suited for memory-intensive applications, in-memory databases, caching layers, and single-threaded data processing tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure FX8-4ms_v2 is a memory-intensive virtual machine from the FXmsv2 family, running on Microsoft Hyper-V. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 4 vCPUs (2 physical cores) operating at 4.2 GHz, supported by a large 260 MB L3 cache. The instance is configured with 168.0 GB of RAM, providing an exceptional 42.0 GB of memory per core, though it lacks local storage and GPUs. Benchmarks show top-tier single-core and text processing performance, alongside strong single-threaded compression speeds. Conversely, multi-core and large-block memory bandwidth benchmarks are weaker due to the low core count. This resource profile makes the server highly cost-efficient for memory-bound, low-concurrency workloads. It is best suited for in-memory databases, caching layers, and single-threaded data processing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure FX8-4ms_v2 is a memory-intensive VM featuring 4 vCPUs, 168 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8573C CPU at 4.2 GHz. It delivers top-tier single-core performance for memory-bound workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure FX8-4ms_v2 is a memory-intensive VM with 4 vCPUs, 168 GB RAM, and a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, ideal for in-memory databases.
tagline: A memory-intensive virtual machine featuring an exceptional memory-to-core ratio and strong single-threaded performance for specialized, memory-bound workloads.

168.0 GB of RAM providing 42.0 GB per core
